title: The install ceremony has no reflected CLI op — a CLI-driven bootstrap reaches a config-complete-but-unmaterialized substrate with no CLI op to advance it
description: installContext is surfaced to the operator only as the /context install slash-command handler and is listed in INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS, so the reflecting CLI has no context-install op while its five lifecycle siblings (context-init / context-accept-all / context-switch / context-list / context-archive) are reflected ops. A CLI-only operator runs context-init + context-accept-all to a config-complete substrate whose schemas/ is empty and whose block files do not exist, with no CLI op to materialize them; the only non-ad-hoc completion is dropping to a pi -p "/context install" subprocess. Class: substrate-lifecycle operations unevenly reflected to the CLI — the CLI-parity family FGAP-030 named (FGAP-021..026 + FGAP-030); install is the last unreflected member of the init -> accept-all -> install bootstrap triad, so the CLI bootstrap is structurally incompletable on its own surface. The INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS rationale (install is operator-facing via the command handler, not the op-registry) is the assertion to revisit; FGAP-030 set the precedent that a command-only surface unreachable from the CLI is a gap resolved by adding the reflected op.

evidence.reference: installContext(cwd, {overwrite}) — the install engine: copies installed_schemas[] bodies into <substrate>/schemas/, installed_blocks[] starters into <substrate>/<name>.json, base-stamps install baselines
the install entry of CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS — installContext is surfaced ONLY as the /context install slash-command handler
installContext listed in INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS with the rationale that it is operator-facing via the command handler, not the op-registry — the stale assertion this gap revisits
the full investigation: root cause, the init+accept-all-then-empty-schemas/ repro, the pi -p seam, the FGAP-030 precedent, the class verdict

impact: A CLI-driven or agent-driven (non-pi) consumer cannot bootstrap a substrate to a usable state: init + accept-all leave schemas/ empty and no block files, and a subsequent append-block-item --autoId throws 'nextId: schema not found' (block-api.ts:2311). The capability is reachable only by dropping to a pi -p "/context install" subprocess.
canonical_vocabulary: CLI bootstrap completability / lifecycle-op reflection parity
proposed_resolution: Determined by the implementing pass (Experience-Gap Handling): reflect install as a context-install OpDefinition like its lifecycle siblings (writer-classified via the DEC-0006 ctx/writer channel that context-init/context-accept-all already carry), revisiting the INTENTIONALLY_UNEXPOSED_WRITERS entry. Verification: a pure-CLI context-init -> context-accept-all -> context-install bootstrap materializes schemas + block files with no pi -p subprocess. Grounding: analysis/2026-06-13-install-surface-and-guard-scope-gaps.md.
